Definition: Skills in managing stakeholder relationships, ensuring business alignment, and securing executive buy-in
Capabilities regarding team leadership, mentorship, motivation, and cross-functional collaboration
Demonstration of business acumen, strategic thinking, product strategy, and commercial awareness
Competence in project planning, scheduling, resource allocation, and timeline management
Proficiency in established project management frameworks, methodologies, and lifecycles (e.g., Agile, SAFe, Waterfall)
Ability to identify, assess, mitigate, and manage delivery risks and dependencies
Foundational theoretical knowledge of machine learning, data science concepts
Knowledge of operational infrastructure, deployment pipelines, and CI/CD practices for ML models (MLOps)
Capacity to adapt and thrive within fast-paced, ambiguous, or rapidly evolving environments
Driving organizational change, user adoption, and upskilling regarding AI literacy and tools
Practical programming skills and proficiency with standard data science and engineering tools (e.g., Python, SQL, Amazon Web Services AWS)
Responsibility for financial oversight, budget management, forecasting, and cost control
Proficiency in conflict resolution, negotiation, and navigating ambiguity
Understanding of architectures, theories, and concepts specific to Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs)
Technical proficiency with specific project management and collaboration software (e.g., Jira, Asana, Linear)
